mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in real retail sales indicates sustained consumer demand, which directly supports corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the rolling 12-month correlation between RSAFS growth rate and 20-day forward SPX returns is not positive (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below the 5.0% threshold, it confirms a tightening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, typically generating positive equity returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward SPX return in periods following UNRATE < 5.0% is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or underperforms buy-and-hold over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and GDP growth signals broad-based economic strength, reducing recession probability and supporting medium-term equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint signal (both series above 6-month moving averages) does not produce positive average 60-day returns or the interaction term in a linear regression is insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or below 0.5%, the market expects continued monetary accommodation, which compresses discount rates and supports higher equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward SPX return during FEDFUNDS <= 0.5% periods is not significantly higher than during other periods (two-sample t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity, a leading indicator for economic slowdown that historically drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the contemporaneous correlation between monthly INDPRO changes and 5-day forward returns is not negative (Spearman r < -0.1) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate above 0.5% marks the beginning of a tightening cycle, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward return following a rate rise above 0.5% is not significantly negative (t-test p < 0.05) or the strategy yields negative Sharpe over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation and unemployment, consistent with a stagflationary regime, hurt corporate margins and consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward SPX return during months when both CPIAUCSL and UNRATE rise month-over-month is not significantly negative (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In the early phase of an economic recovery, robust GDP growth combined with rising housing starts signals durable expansion, supporting strong long-term equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and HOUST both above their 12-month lows
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return during indicated recovery periods is not statistically higher than the unconditional mean (one-sample t-test p < 0.05) or if the regime captures fewer than 10 non-overlapping instances in history.
